item.title: Zimbabwe Gold Exports at Risk From Escalating Middle East War
item.description: Zimbabwe’s key gold exports are vulnerable to an escalation in the Middle East conflict, according to a business lobby.

item.title: Santander’s Botin Sees Efficiency Improving in First Quarter
item.description: Banco Santander SA Executive Chair Ana Botin said the lender’s efficiency will improve in the current quarter and she also said profit will increase for the year as a whole.

item.title: US Gets Another Magnet Plant in Push to Tackle China’s Dominance
item.description: USA Rare Earth Inc. will start commercial shipments of magnets from April, adding to a handful of projects that aim to curb America’s reliance on China for the vital industrial components.

item.title: UK Retail Sales Post First Drop in Three Months, ONS Says
item.description: UK retail sales declined for the first time since November, with consumers reining in their spending even before the Iran war clouded Britain’s economic outlook.

item.title: Hong Kong Mulls Tax Exemption for Fund Managers to Boost Hub
item.description: Hong Kong is close to proposing a tax regime change that will exempt private equity and potentially hedge fund managers from paying tax on their earnings from carried interest and performance fees.

item.title: India’s Rupee at Fresh Low as Outflows Accelerate Amid Oil Shock
item.description: India’s rupee weakened to a fresh low, lagging behind regional peers, as foreign investors sped up sales of stocks and bonds on fears that elevated energy prices would drive up the import-heavy nation’s inflation and import bill.

item.title: Pound Steady After February Retail Sales Fell Less Than Expected
France Beats Deficit Target, Giving Leeway in Energy Crisis
item.description: France beat its 2025 deficit reduction target, giving the government some margin for maneuver as the fallout from the Iran war casts a cloud over plans to keep repairing public finances.

item.title: German Engine Maker’s Defense Pivot to Yield Revenue Jump
item.description: Deutz AG sees double-digit revenue growth for 2026 as the agricultural engine maker pushes deeper into defense, positioning itself to benefit from Europe’s rearmament drive.

item.title: South African President Backtracks on US Role in G7 Summit Snub
item.description: South Africa walked back its claim that US pressure led to its disinvitation from an upcoming Group of Seven summit in France.

item.title: AI Breakthrough From Google Exposes Divide in Memory Chip Stocks
item.description: A two-day selloff in memory-chip stocks is revealing a split in the artificial intelligence trade, as Google touts a breakthrough that analysts say may curb demand for certain types of storage while leaving others largely unscathed.

item.title: Japan Says Oil Reserves for Domestic Use Amid Asia Pleas for Aid
item.description: Japan’s trade minister said the country will sell oil from its reserves to domestic refiners as a general rule, signaling that the government isn’t currently planning to channel national supplies directly to other Asian nations seeking assistance.

item.title: Europe’s Economy Is Starting to Feel Pain From Trump’s Iran War
item.description: The economic toll of the Iran war is hitting home in Europe, where more muted growth and faster inflation risk deepening industrial, fiscal and political pressures across the region.

item.title: Brace for $200 Oil If the War Lasts Till June, Macquarie Warns
item.description: Oil may hit a record $200 a barrel if the Iran war drags on till June, with the Strait of Hormuz staying shut, Macquarie Group Ltd. said.

item.title: Oil Falls as Trump Extends Deadline Again for Iran to Make a Deal
item.description: Sunni and Shia Muslim clerics at a rally organised by Lebanese political parties supporting the Hezbollah movement outside the Iranian embassy in Beirut on March 26. Photographer: ANWAR AMRO/AFP

item.title: London Urgently Needs New Homes. Why Can’t It Build Them?
item.description: A mix of economic and bureaucratic factors mean that home construction in the UK capital has largely stalled out.

item.title: Iran War Lifts Key Japanese Aluminum Premium to 11-Year High
item.description: Japan’s aluminum buyers agreed to pay the highest premium in 11 years after the Iran war disrupted supplies, a cost that’s likely to stoke inflationary pressures at the factories which use the metal.

item.title: Two Trades Likely Drove Biggest Indonesia Outflow in 21 Years
item.description: Indonesian stocks recorded their biggest foreign outflow in more than two decades, likely driven by block trades in palm oil maker PT FAP Agri, amid heightened scrutiny on ownership in the wake of MSCI Inc.’s investability concerns.

item.title: Singapore Looks to Host Central-Bank Gold Reserves to Boost Hub
item.description: Singapore is planning to expand its gold-storage capacity to become a custodian of bullion held by foreign central banks, part of a broader push by the city-state to compete with Hong Kong as a regional hub for the precious metal.

item.title: India Cuts Fuel Taxes to Shield Refiners From Iran War Impact
item.description: India has cut taxes on diesel and gasoline to cushion the impact of surging crude prices on its refiners, as a deepening conflict in the Middle East disrupts global supply.

item.title: Copper Heads for First Weekly Gain Since Start of Iran War
item.description: Copper rose on Friday — and was headed for its first weekly gain this month — on optimism that US efforts to end the war in Middle East would bear fruit and prevent a slowdown in global growth.

item.title: Treasurer Says Philippine Central Bank Hold May Calm Bond Market
item.description: The Philippine central bank’s decision to hold off raising interest rates on Thursday may help stabilize the government bond market after yields surged since the start of the Iran war, the country’s national treasurer said.

item.title: China’s March LNG Imports Poised for 8-Year Low as Prices Spike
item.description: China’s liquefied natural gas imports this month are on pace to hit the lowest level since 2018, according to ship-tracking data compiled by Kpler, as a price spike caused by the conflict in the Middle East curbs demand.

item.title: Australia Ramps Up Private Credit Scrutiny, Demanding More Data
item.description: Australia’s corporate regulator is boosting supervision of the private credit industry by requesting more detailed data from funds on a weekly basis as scrutiny grows on the $1.8 trillion industry worldwide.

item.title: Japan to Allow More Coal-Fired Power to Cope With Energy Shock
item.description: Japan will allow more use of coal-fired power plants in an effort to boost security of supply to cope with the energy shock from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Rupee Emerges as Achilles Heel for India Stock Bulls
item.description: Recent currency gains likely to be temporary unless external pressure eases.

item.title: Innovent Shares Jump on First Profit, Obesity Drug Momentum
item.description: Innovent Biologics Inc. shares saw their biggest gains in nearly five months, after the Chinese drugmaker posted its first annual profit thanks to surging demand for its weight-loss drug.

item.title: China Solar Wrap: Polysilicon Prices Decline on Bearish Outlook
item.description: Chinese polysilicon prices fell for a fourth week on persistent bearish sentiment around the supply and demand outlook.

item.title: DBS Joins Buzzing India ECM Space With Mandate in $1 Billion IPO
item.description: DBS Group Holdings Ltd. has entered India’s buoyant equity capital market, marking a push into one of the world’s busiest venues for share sales.

item.title: Ping An of China Prefers Short-Term Debt to Dodge Iran War Risk
item.description: Ping An of China Asset Management (Hong Kong) plans to ramp up purchases of short-term debt issued by Chinese banks to shield its investments from market volatility sparked by the Iran war.
